options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: death_link
options.description: When you die, everyone who enabled death link dies. Of course, the reverse is true too.

options.name: energy_link
options.description:
Enable EnergyLink support.

EnergyLink in MMX2 works as a big HP and Weapon Energy pool that the players can use to request HP
or Weapon Energy whenever they need to.

You make use of this feature by typing /heal <amount> or /refill <amount> in the client.

options.name: button_configuration
options.description: Default buttons for every action.

options.name: pickupsanity
options.description: Whether collecting freestanding 1ups, HP and Weapon Energy capsules will grant a check.

options.name: starting_life_count
options.description:
How many lives to start the game with.

Note: This number becomes the new default life count, meaning that it will persist after a game over.

options.name: starting_hp
options.description:
How much HP X will have at the start of the game.

Notes: Going over 32 HP may cause visual bugs in either gameplay or the pause menu. The max HP is capped at 56.

options.name: heart_tank_effectiveness
options.description:
How many units of HP each Heart tank will provide to the user.

Note: Going over 32 HP may cause visual bugs in either gameplay or the pause menu. The max HP is capped at 56.

options.name: jammed_buster
options.description:
Jams X's buster making it only able to shoot lemons.
Note: This adds another Arms Upgrade into the item pool.

options.name: zsaber_in_pool
options.description:
Adds Z-Saber to the item pool.

Z-Saber melee attack will deal 100% HP as DMG, ranged attack will deal 50% HP and its DoT will deal 4x2 DMG.
On strict weakness settings Z-Saber will deal half the DMG (doesn't affect DoT from ranged attack)

options.name: quick_charge_in_pool
options.description:
Adds Quick Charge Chip from Mega Man X5 into the item pool.

Halves charge time for X-Buster and Special Weapon shots.

options.name: speedster_in_pool
options.description:
Adds Speedster Chip from Mega Man X5 into the item pool.

Increases walking speed by 50%

options.name: super_recover_in_pool
options.description:
Adds Super Recover Chip from Mega Man X5 into the item pool.

Increases recovery from items by 25%. Also affects EnergyLink deposit rate. Doesn't affect Sub Tanks.

options.name: rapid_five_in_pool
options.description:
Adds Rapid Five Chip from Mega Man X5 into the item pool.

Increases the amount of buster shots on screen to 5.

options.name: speed_shot_in_pool
options.description:
Adds Speed Shot Chip from Mega Man X5 into the item pool.

Increases X's buster shots (lemons) horizontal speed by 50%.

options.name: buster_plus_in_pool
options.description:
Adds Buster Plus Chip from Mega Man X5 into the item pool.

Increases Buster (lemon) damage by 1.

options.name: weapon_plus_in_pool
options.description:
Adds Weapon Plus Chip from Mega Man X6 into the item pool.

Increases Weapon damage by 1.

options.name: item_plus_in_pool
options.description:
Adds Item Plus Chip from Mega Man X7 into the item pool.

Increases item drop rates by roughly 33%.

options.name: disable_charge_freeze
options.display_name: Disable Level 3 Charge freeze after shooting
options.description: Allows X and Zero to move while shooting a level 3 charged shot.

options.name: long_jumps
options.description: Allows X to perform longer jumps when holding down the Dash button. Only works after getting a Legs Upgrade.

options.name: boss_weakness_rando
options.description:
Every main boss will have its weakness randomized.

vanilla: Bosses retain their original weaknesses
shuffled: Bosses have their weaknesses shuffled
chaotic_double: Bosses will have two random weaknesses under the chaotic set
chaotic_single: Bosses will have one random weakness under the chaotic set

The chaotic set makes every weapon charge level a separate weakness instead of keeping
them together, meaning that a boss can be weak to Charged Frost Shield but not its
uncharged version.

options.name: boss_weakness_strictness
options.description:
How strict boss weaknesses will be.

not_strict: Allow every weapon to deal damage to the bosses
weakness_and_buster: Only allow the weakness and buster to deal damage to the bosses
weakness_and_upgraded_buster: Only allow the weakness and buster charge levels 3 & 4 to deal damage to the bosses
only_weakness: Only the weakness will deal damage to the bosses

options.name: boss_weakness_plando
options.description:
Forces bosses to have a specific weakness. Uses the names that appear on the chaotic weakness set.

Format: 
  Boss Name: Weakness Name

options.name: boss_randomize_hp
options.description:
Wheter to randomize the boss' hp or not.

off: Bosses' HP will not be randomized
weak: Bosses will have [1,32] HP
regular: Bosses will have [16,48] HP
strong: Bosses will have [32,64] HP
chaotic: Bosses will have [1,64] HP

options.name: logic_boss_weakness
options.description:
Most bosses will logically expect you to have its weakness.

This option will be forced if the Boss Weakness Strictness setting is set to require only the weakness or
the upgraded buster option.

options.name: doppler_open
options.description:
Under which conditions will Dr. Doppler's lab open.
If no options are selected a multiworld item granting access to the lab will be created.

Medals: Consider Maverick medals to get access to the lab.
Weapons: Consider weapons to get access to the lab.
Armor Upgrades: Consider upgrades to get access to the lab.
Heart Tanks: Consider heart tanks to get access to the lab.
Sub Tanks: Consider sub tanks to get access to the lab.

options.name: doppler_medal_count
options.description: How many Maverick Medals are required to access Dr. Doppler's Lab.

options.name: doppler_weapon_count
options.description: How many weapons are required to access Dr. Doppler's Lab.

options.name: doppler_upgrade_count
options.description: How many armor upgrades are required to access Dr. Doppler's Lab.

options.name: doppler_heart_tank_count
options.description: How many Heart Tanks are required to access Dr. Doppler's Lab.

options.name: doppler_sub_tank_count
options.description: How many Sub Tanks are required to access Dr. Doppler's Lab.

options.name: doppler_lab_2_boss
options.description:
Which boss will appear in the second Dr Doppler's Lab stage.

Note: Also affects the stage variation.

options.name: doppler_lab_3_boss_rematch_count
options.description: How many boss rematches are needed in the third Dr. Doppler's Lab stage.

options.name: doppler_all_labs
options.description:
Whether to unlock Dr. Doppler's Lab 1-3 levels as a group or not.

Unlocking level 4 requires getting all Lab levels cleared.

options.name: logic_vile_required
options.display_name: Vile in logic for Lab Access
options.description:
Adds a logic check for Dr. Doppler's Lab access so that it expects Vile to be defeated before accessing it.

Note: It does not affect the actual Dr. Doppler's Lab access options.

options.name: vile_open
options.description:
Under which conditions will Vile's Stage open.
If no options are selected a multiworld item granting access to the stage will be created.

Medals: Consider Maverick medals to get access to the lab.
Weapons: Consider weapons to get access to the lab.
Armor Upgrades: Consider upgrades to get access to the lab.
Heart Tanks: Consider heart tanks to get access to the lab.
Sub Tanks: Consider sub tanks to get access to the lab.

options.name: vile_medal_count
options.description: How many Maverick Medals are required to access Vile's Stage.

options.name: vile_weapon_count
options.description: How many weapons are required to access Vile's Stage.

options.name: vile_upgrade_count
options.description: How many armor upgrades are required to access Vile's Stage.

options.name: vile_heart_tank_count
options.description: How many Heart Tanks are required to access Vile's Stage.

options.name: vile_sub_tank_count
options.description: How many Sub Tanks are required to access Vile's Stage.

options.name: bit_medal_count
options.description: How many Maverick Medals are required to access Bit's fight.

options.name: byte_medal_count
options.description:
How many Maverick Medals are required to access Byte's fight.

Note: If Byte's medal count is less than or equal to Bit's, the value will be adjusted to Bit's + 1.

options.name: player_palettes
options.description:
Allows you to create colors for each weapon X has. Includes charge levels and Gold Armor customization.
This will override the option preset

Each one expects 16 values which are mapped to X's colors.
The values can be in SNES RGB (bgr555) with the $ prefix or PC RGB (rgb888) with the # prefix.

options.name: palette_default
options.description: Which color to use for X's default color

options.name: palette_gold_armor
options.description: Which color to use for X's Gold Armor

options.name: palette_charge_blue
options.description: Which color to use for X's Level 1 & 2 Charge

options.name: palette_charge_pink
options.description: Which color to use for X's Level 3 Charge

options.name: palette_charge_red
options.description: Which color to use for X's Level 4 Charge

options.name: palette_charge_green
options.description: Which color to use for X's Z-Saber Charge

options.name: palette_acid_burst
options.description: Which color to use for X's Acid Burst

options.name: palette_parasitic_bomb
options.description: Which color to use for X's Parasitic Bomb

options.name: palette_triad_thunder
options.description: Which color to use for X's Triad Thunder

options.name: palette_spinning_blade
options.description: Which color to use for X's Spinning Blade

options.name: palette_ray_splasher
options.description: Which color to use for X's Ray Splasher

options.name: palette_gravity_well
options.description: Which color to use for X's Gravity Well

options.name: palette_frost_shield
options.description: Which color to use for X's Frost Shield

options.name: palette_tornado_fang
options.description: Which color to use for X's Tornado Fang

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.

options.name: non_local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be outside their native world.

options.name: start_inventory
options.description: Start with the specified amount of these items. Example: "Bomb: 1"

options.name: start_hints
options.description: Start with these item's locations prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: start_location_hints
options.description: Start with these locations and their item prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: item_links
options.description: Share part of your item pool with other players.

options.name: plando_items
options.description: Generic items plando.

options.name: start_inventory_from_pool
options.description:
Start with the specified amount of these items and don't place them in the world. Example: "Bomb: 1"

The game decides what the replacement items will be.
